summary: Create acceptable document set model
description:
• Creates a new acceptable document set with a first draft version.
  • The new version initializes with the provided data, has the default status of draft and the author as the only required signee.


Required permissions:
Following permissions are required: DocumentConfigurationEdit

summary: Create acceptable document set version by version number
description:
• Creates a new version from an existing acceptable document version.
  • Assigns the next available version number.
  • The new version initializes with the provided data, has the default status of draft and the author as the only required signee.


Required permissions:
Following permissions are required: DocumentConfigurationEdit

summary: Update acceptable document set version
description:
Updates an existing acceptable document set version. Only draft and rejected versions are allowed to be updated.

Required permissions:
Following permissions are required: DocumentConfigurationEdit

summary: Delete acceptable document set version
description:
• Deletes the selected version of an existing acceptable document set.
  • If the version is the last one, then the version number of it will become the next available one for the model. Otherwise, the version number will be flagged as deleted and become unavailable.


Required permissions:
Following permissions are required: DocumentConfigurationDelete

summary: Create acceptable document set version with given acceptable documents

summary: Save document metadata and generate the URL to upload the file - CreateDocumentModel

This method will create the document model metadata and generate a signed URL to allow the user to upload the actual file directly in the AWS S3 bucket. The method will return an object with some basic information to show the result to the user in the UI like status, document requirement and document type. The response also contains the URL to upload the file in the S3 bucket.

At least one of the following permissions is required: DocumentManagementCreate, DocumentManagementEdit

summary: Save temporary document metadata and generate the URL to upload the file for multiple Document Requirements
description:
This method will create temporary document model metadata and generate a signed URL to allow the user to upload temporary file directly in the AWS S3 bucket. When multiple document Requirements need to be fulfilled Important: The returned `SignedUrlToUpload` is a presigned S3 URL that includes the `x-amz-tagging` header in its signature. When uploading the file via HTTP PUT to this URL, the caller must include the following header exactly as shown: ```x-amz-tagging: TTL=enabled``` Omitting this header or providing a different value will result in a `403 SignatureDoesNotMatch` error from S3, because the header is part of the presigned URL's HMAC signature.

Required permissions:
At least one of the following permissions is required: DocumentManagementCreate, DocumentManagementEdit
